title: Traveling with Kyle - Guyana Part 2, Suriname

transcript.text: I'm taking a long time, aren't I? So yes, we're driving down the jungle and
I'm sitting on top of the truck, but since we're driving through the jungle
There's all these trees that would overgrow over the road
And at this point the road was in a lot better shape. It was a lot drier and I guess better
Maintained so the truck was going faster. There weren't those really huge divots in the earth like anymore
It was it was just kind of like driving down a bumpy dirt road, and then at one point just a normal dirt road, but
So they're driving down the road and maybe
But they're still not going so fast maybe 20 miles an hour
And I was sitting on the top of because it's in the jungle. There's all this growth. It's like foliage going over the top
So I'd have to look out and whenever we were coming up on a tree
I'd have to run back and then hide behind the truck
And let the trees go over and then I jumped back on the roof again
And I had to do that like every like minute like it was on a regular basis, but it didn't really bother me because it was kind of exercise
You know, and it was cool to be out there
But at one point I forgot and I was like oh shit. It was too late
So I had to like hold on to the strap and it just like
Scratched me pretty good actually
But uh, yeah at one point I was looking forward and I saw this Jaguar
Walk right in front of the truck
Just really mellow not trying to run away from the truck or anything. I guess the new
He'd seen those trucks a lot
It was pretty
Um
And yeah on the way down I also saw that black guy again on the way back
Just walking down the road like a miles away from there, so I guess he wasn't afraid of Jaguars
Although they did have a lot of Jaguar sags. I read in the newspaper when I was at that truck stock place that uh
Some person who was like in Georgetown or something some person came home and they saw a Jaguar and their front lawn in their front porch sitting there
So he ran and got his gun and shot it
Because I guess sometimes I guess one of them attacked somebody and killed him or something like that, but uh
Yeah, so then we drive and then we get to where it was paved road and we drove down and I
Bought this kid dinner again, and then we went into oh, but anything he had me stay at his brother's house
Who I guess it was his his and his brother's house or something but his brother and his wife and I think they had like a baby or something
And I stayed there for like two nights I think and they gave me my own room my own bed
It was pretty cool, but it was like totally
Dilapidated house like just a couple of rooms and all the you know the boards the floorboards were just like rotting and coming out and
Um
And his but it was weird because his brother was kind of acting like
Wasn't happy to see him at all and they weren't talking at all the only person who said anything the whole time was the kid
I was with and he'd be like oh, yeah great to see my brother great to see my brother
And that's all he'd say and
But uh the brother told me when I went out go around town to check it out. He'd be like yeah
Well, and I think I even hung out at the house by myself, but he's like yeah
Don't let anybody in the house
So
So
That's weird
But uh, yeah, georgetown is definitely probably
It's definitely a georgetown is the seediest poorest most dangerous looking capital city. I've ever been to
There were a lot georgetown was part of the english empire and they had all these in the center part
They have all these I guess a couple of english churches
English buildings
Actually english style houses, but they're built a long time ago and all the houses in georgetown were just dilapidated
Haven't been painted for 50 years. The wood was rotted and I just
Warped and

Um
Oh, yeah, of course my buddy

I don't I think he said something about how he had money
In some other place and he'd be able to pay me then but

All these people playing cricket in the field

These little uh
